meaning: adj.忙于琐事的,无关紧要的n.行商
examples: He worked as a door-to-door salesman peddling cloths and brushes.
Peddling a handful can lead to a 15-year sentence.
They said peddling was the best job they could find.
And no license fees (or very small ones) when it comes to peddling these knockoffs.
ComScore recently counted 1.4 million unique monthly visitors to the advice-peddling site, which has 490, 000 members.
